entry.title: Rearrange cells in a sap.m.ColumnListItem
entry.content:
I have a freestyle SAPUI5 application with a sap.m.Table. The Table is defined in an XML view and its "items" aggregation contains a single sap.m.ColumnListItem which contain 10 cells. The cells are fairly complex, several of them contain multiple Controls.

I wanted to implement Personalization (using the sap.m.p13n.Engine available since 1.115). SAP has several samples in the SAPUI5 docs but all are very basic in handling the sap.m.ColumnListItem cells. Whenever you change the Personalization, the sample creates a new sap.m.ColumnListItem object with new sap.m.Text objects.

Unfortunately, my case is not that simple. My Table definition is over 300 lines in the XML view. It would be an overkill to convert all that to JS code, so that at every Personalization change I recreate my entire Table from JS code.

So my question is, what is the correct way to rearrange the cells in a ColumnListItem from JS code? I tried about 6-7 different approaches but none of them worked 100%. I faced problems like

  • I rearrange the cells in the ColumnListItem (with removeCell and addCell) but in the browser the cells are not rearranged.
  • If I create a new ColumnListItem, I cannot create it with the old ID, because the old ColumnListItem still exists somewhere.
  • Sometimes when I tried to add the cells in a new place it is impossible because the cells were already destroyed.

I guess, performance wise the most ideal method would be to reuse both the cells (and not recreate them every time) and ideally reuse the ColumnListItem.

entry.title: Re: Inventory Stock Reversal in SAP: Best Practices for O...
entry.content:
This is an example of a batch request in SAP oData:

--batch_36522ad7-fc75-4b56-8c71-56071383e77b
Content-Type: application/http
Content-Transfer-Encoding:binary GET serviceRoot/Airlines HTTP/1.1
Accept: application/json;odata.metadata=minimal --batch_36522ad7-fc75-4b56-8c71-56071383e77b
Content-Type: application/http
Content-Transfer-Encoding:binary
Content-ID: 1 POST serviceRoot/Airlines HTTP/1.1
OData-Version: 4.0
Content-Type: application/json;odata.metadata=minimal
Accept: application/json;odata.metadata=minimal {
"@odata.type" : "Microsoft.OData.SampleService.Models.TripPin.Airline",
"AirlineCode" : "EK",
"Name" : "Emirates Airline"
} --batch_36522ad7-fc75-4b56-8c71-56071383e77b
Content-Type: application/http
Content-Transfer-Encoding:binary GET serviceRoot/Airlines HTTP/1.1
Accept: application/json;odata.metadata=minimal --batch_36522ad7-fc75-4b56-8c71-56071383e77b--

Source:oData Docs 

What this does:
Batch requests allow grouping multiple operations into a single HTTP request payload. The Batch request below contains the following operations in order listed:
1. A query request, returns all Airlines.
2. Create an entity, a new Airline.
3. A second query request Airlines.

Here is another batch request example:

--batch_36522ad7-fc75-4b56-8c71-56071383e77b
Content-Type: application/http
Content-Transfer-Encoding:binary
GET /b1s/v1/Items('i001')
--batch_36522ad7-fc75-4b56-8c71-56071383e77b
Content-Type: multipart/mixed;boundary=changeset_77162fcd-b8da-41ac-a9f8-9357efbbd
--changeset_77162fcd-b8da-41ac-a9f8-9357efbbd
Content-Type: application/http
Content-Transfer-Encoding: binary
Content-ID: 1
POST /b1s/v1/Items('i002')
Content-Type: application/json
<Json format item(i002) body>
--changeset_77162fcd-b8da-41ac-a9f8-9357efbbd
Content-Type: application/http
Content-Transfer-Encoding: binary
Content-ID: 2
PATCH /b1s/v1/$1
Content-Type: application/json
<Json format item(i002) update body>
--changeset_77162fcd-b8da-41ac-a9f8-9357efbbd--
--batch_36522ad7-fc75-4b56-8c71-56071383e77b--

Source: Working with SAP bs1 PDF 

This batch request does the following:
1. Gets Item with code i001
2. It then creates a new item and assigns Content-ID of 1
3. It then updates taht new item (with content id of 1) and assigns Content-ID of 2

What am I want to achieve now with batch requests is the following:
1. Cancel an Incoming Payment
2. Create a Creditnote on the specified invoice
3. Cancel a Sales Order based of the Invoice

This is the endpoint to cancel an Invoice: /IncomingPayments(id)/Cancel (POST request)
The incoming payment's Id I will specify dynamically.

This is the endpoint to create a Creditnote: /CreditNotes (POST request)
and here is an EXAMPLE request body:

{
 "CardCode":"TKI_ELLN",
 "JournalMemo": "Invoice Cancelled",
 "DocumentLines":[
  {
   "ItemCode":"6002",
   "UnitPrice": 55.0,
   "Quantity": 1.0,
   "WarehouseCode":"TKI_ELL",
   "BaseEntry": 26443,
   "BaseType": 13,
   "BaseLine": 0
  }
 ]
}

The following properties should be dynamically specified:
CardCode, ItemCode, UnitPrice, Quantity, WarehouseCode, BaseEntry.

At the time of writing, I realize this won't be possible 🥲. Because I don't know if batch request support for loops.

I continue anyway...

This is the endpoint to cancel an Order: /Orders(id)/Cancel (POST request)
The order Id I will specify dynamically as well.

entry.title: ERR InsufficientResources - Insufficient resources in BTP Trial CF
entry.content:
Hi colleagues,

I was trying to deploy a python based app multiple times. After each failed attempt I deleted the app in the BTP subaccount's Space. After about 5 or so attempts, the app did not even get staged anymore, instead I get the message "InsufficientResources - Insufficient resources" 

My gut feeling tells me, there are containers being created in the background with each attempted push. Deleting the app from th espace after failed deployments may delete the content of the container, but not the container itself. After a few failed attempts empty containers hog all the space / memory. Or, the container does get deleted, but for some reason the system still believes there is a container that needs to allocate memory. Which is also not helpful.   

How may I delete empty containers and free scarce memory in my Trial space?

entry.title: Re: From where the recipients are fetched in case of SWWE...
entry.content:
Hello,

Maybe it is too late, but I just discovered where recipients came from: 

Class: CL_SWF_RUN_JOB_WIM_LOCAL
Method: IF_SWF_RUN_WIM~GET_ADMINISTRATOR_AGENTS

There you will see following comment: 
* get WF ADMINISTRATOR  
* 1. ... from DEFINITION of the flowitem                              
* 2. ... from CUSTOMIZING ( SWDC )                                      
* 3. ... take WF_INITIATOR                                           
* 4. ... take SY-UNAME                                                
 
Thanks!
Lourdes

https://help.sap.com/doc/a2b4c7ef61d64397b2b1d052082a8a08/2402/en-US/PSM_ISI_R_II_TIMX_TIME_ACCT_INFO_REQU_IB.htm

entry.title: Re: Wildfly resource adapter for SAP JRA and sapjco3
entry.content:
The SAP Java Resource Adapter (JRA) is a pre-installed J2EE connector in the SAP NetWeaver Application Server Java. It is not available separately for installing it into other 3rd party J2EE Web Application Server environments.

Extracting/copying sapjra.jar from an SAP NW AS Java installation and using it outside of this environment, is not supported and I think, is also a violation of your SAP license.

Use SAP Java Connector 3.1 directly instead. Anyway, it offers better performance, more features and is easier to handle compared to JRA.

entry.title: Re: PR Generated For Obsolete Materials
entry.content: Hi, Check for the request date on item level Quantities/dates tab, or EBAN field BADAT for created date. Also check whether the PR has been generated through MRP, if the created indicator is B in the contact person tab at the PR item level (Or EBAN field ESTKZ) You can check the dates against, the material status changed date. Also, as I have explained earlier, try deleting the PR and execute MRP again to see if a new PR is generated after the next MRP runs. If the PR has not been fixed (Fixed ID indicator at quantities/dates tab), normally it should delete and recreate new PR, if MRP is executed with planning mode 3 without a block. Also, if the material status blocks the purchasing also, you will get error message “blocked for procurement” in converting the PR to PO.

entry.title: Automatic Creation of One Time Payments from Time Collectors Using Integration Center
entry.content:
Introduction

This blog is a continuation of my previous article where I have explained in detail on how to automate the absence creation in SuccessFactors Time using the capabilities of the integration center. You may want to read through it before we go through this blog since I would reference a lot of configuration parts that are explained in detail in that article. You can read it here.

The question that came up after the community went through the blog was - "How do we send the cumulated time collector values to payroll as SF Time Tracking does not allow for a time collector to be an input group to generate a time pay type nor are you able to assign a time collector as also a time pay type group"

The answer lies in the fact that we cannot look at SF Time Tracking as a standalone product. SAP has designed it intelligently to work together with the Employee Central Payroll module. The capabilities we seek are inherent to these two solutions and need to be leveraged as such. However, while I detail both the approaches to send the values to EC payroll, the focus of this blog is to explain how to auto-create a time pay component into the one time payments portlet in EC and thereby allow for the standard PTP replication to take over and send to payroll for a payout. 

We are going to explore the possibilities of using time collectors to send the cumulated values to payroll or to send the time pay types to payroll and cumulate there. The technical flow diagram is as below:

Scenario:

Let us take a simple client scenario wherein whenever an employee is late to work / leaves early from work, the system needs to send the late coming/ early leaving hours to EC Payroll for deduction. Simple, isn't it? I will explain both the solutions - wherein, the hours are sent to EC Payroll as soon as they are created in terms of the time pay types upon time valuation and also if we want to cumulate them into a time collector and then send them across to payroll. 

Firstly, lets tackle the generic steps that are used to create the time pay types and the time collector values in SuccessFactors Time Tracking module. 

Recap from my previous blog - Technical Solution Steps to achieve the above client scenario

Step 1: Create a Time Recording Profile:

In Manage Data – create a time recording profile with positive time recording method and a time recording variant of clock times

 Step 2: Create time type groups for planned working time, recorded working time and to hold the difference between the planned and recorded working times

Step 2.1: Create time type group for planned working time


Step 2.2: Create time type group for recorded working time


Step 2.3: Create time type group to hold the difference between planned and actual working time


Step 3: Create a valuation rule to calculate the difference between the planned and recorded working time for identifying the gaps between the planned and recorded working time of an employee


Step 4: Create a time type group that would be used to identify the gaps within the start of recorded working time and start of the planned working time.

Note: A time collector will be used to store this value for creating an automatic one time payment through integration center to showcase the technical ability for sending the cumulated value to Employee Central Payroll

Step 5: Create a valuation rule that will filter the gaps between the start of the planned working time and the recorded working time.

Step 6: Create a time type group that identifies the gaps in the end time of the planned and recorded working times and collects it for auto creation of a one time payment through integration center

Step 7: Create a valuation rule that will filter the gaps between the end of the planned working time and the recorded working time.

Step 8: Assign the time valuation(s) to the time recording profile of the employee along with the other rules that may already be assigned to the time recording profile.

Step 9: Assign time recording profile to employee in job information.
Once the time recording profile is assigned to the employee in job information, the time collector value is created after the time sheet entries are made to reflect the scenario of late coming or early leaving.

Options to send the hours to payroll for further processing

Now, let us tackle the two options of the deductions in payroll for the late coming / early leaving. 

A] Time Pay Type containing the difference in the planned versus recorded start/end times sent to EC Payroll basis the time valuation results without the need to use cumulated value in the time collector

This is a simple straightforward solution where the time pay type created with the difference in the planned versus recorded time is pushed to EC Payroll for deduction. In case, there is a limit on the deduction amount per month then it can also be ensured by using the DDNTK functionality and pushing the excess deduction into the following month or generating a claim in the termination month etc. If there is a requirement to check on the quarterly late coming / early leaving hours and alert the payroll manager on the anomaly through validation rules in the payroll control center (PCC), the cumulation can be designed using V_T54C3 table and applying a quarterly cumulation with the resultant wage type being pulled into a customer specific notional result table wage type that can be used for the PCC alerts. 

I would like to highlight a specific scenario where a client's legal requirement is to check for a quarterly overtime capping of 108 hours.

We indeed have a limitation on the quarterly cumulation ability of time collectors and hence the EC Payroll solution is more suitable - the overtime time pay types can be cumulated on the payroll side, the order of capping (highest OT rate to be considered last for capping and the lowest OT rate to considered first for the capping check) can be applied accordingly and then the payment for the overtime can be made in accordance to the 108 hours rule.

We therefore, leverage on the cumulation capability in EC Payroll in V_T54C3 table as below for the various OT wage types. Basis the scenario and design details, you can also use a single wage type that contains the total OT hours for the employee to check against the 108 hours capping - then again, if the capping has priorities based on the payment rates then the below set up will be more ideal. 

The payroll side PCR for this would roughly look like this with complexity increasing as the various business scenarios are applied to it.    

B] Option of sending the cumulated time collector value to payroll through auto creation of the one time payment component in the EC portlet and PTP replication

For the configuration steps for the integration center mapping, please refer to the steps I have already documented in my previous blog here

Field Mapping

We will map the time collector fields created in time valuation to the one - time payment  (EmpPayCompNonRecurring) fields (most important ones) as below: 

Here the pay component has been hard coded as "1111" (which is nothing but the payroll code for the wage type that will then be mapped in the ECP system for the IT 15 input) for this example but this can also be dynamically picked and used basis a lookup created for client specific requirements. This lookup can be used in scenarios where an employee with a different personnel sub area may need a different code. Mind you, this can be segregated in the EC Payroll side in the payroll schema during payroll run. The reason we mention it here is to highlight the technical feasibility of the requirement in SuccessFactors Integration Center + SuccessFactors Time Tracking product. 

You can also apply the conversions from minutes to hours etc. in the calculation section of the integration center. The integration set up now is completed as below:

A sample run will show you the below output for the required employee - this will be the result of the Integration Center job and this will auto create a component in the employee's EC portlet based on the time collector value. 

Hence, based on this Integration Center job that will be scheduled, the time collectors will be used at the end of the frequency period to auto create the hours into a one time payment component for the employee in EC. In our example, this result of 20 will need to reflect in the employee's pay component portlet. 

In this example we have mapped to the amount field but the time collector value can also be mapped to the number field. The payment or deduction definition can also be managed in EC Payroll; essentially the sending of this one time payment to payroll is handled through integration center while the payment/deduction logic can be ascertained basis the scenario use case itself. 

Outcome of the Integration job run therefore creates the 20 units pay component record - wage type 1111 - as below in the EC portlet: 

Conclusion:

There are technical design decisions to be taken by the solution architect on which approach to use and which one's a cleaner design but this blog highlights both the options and provides insights on how to use the capabilities of SF Employee Central, SF Time Tracking, SF Integration Center and SF Employee Central Payroll understanding that all these need to work together seamlessly to ensure the client requirements are met flawlessly.

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – Migrating from groups to spaces and pages - Why, When, and Key Diffe
entry.content:
Hi @Andreea_Zala it is always very helpful to include your S/4HANA release as the options can be different in each release.  

I’m curious to understand your motivation for locking personalization?

Generally personalization aids user productivity and encourages user adoption so locking it risks reducing productivity and frustrating your business users and therefore can damage user adoption.  

We have sometimes seen this happen where IT teams are assuming pages work like groups - pages are actually far more flexible and resilient and changes are easier to manage by the support team as admin changes and personalizations are automatically merged.  But again it depends on your release which features are relevant and helpful. 

Btw Personally I consider /UI2/CONFIG_PERS_OFF to be EXTREMELY harsh and I would never advocate for that.  

In the current release 2023 you might like to try instead launchpad configuration parameter HOMEPAGE_PERSONALIZATION = true

 Other than that please let us know your release and your rationale and we may be able to suggest alternatives

entry.title: Sap Inside Track Bangalore 17th Feb 2024
entry.content:
I got some time this weekend to share my experience as a Volunteer with you all about the experience of very first Sap Inside Track of 2024,On February 17th, the SAP Inside Track Bengaluru once again proved its mettle as an unparalleled hub for knowledge sharing and networking. This event extended a warm welcome to over 1200 attendees, 120+ speakers & 35+ volunteers, offering an enriched platform where learnings were amplified and connections were strengthened. It's been the best SAP Inside Track I've been a part of. This wouldn't have been possible without the hard work and dedication of everyone involved. In this blog post, I will share some personal highlights of the event while celebrating the team that made this successful event possible. So, let's start here.

 

 

Kick-off

As usuall the agenda kick-off started from non other than our known Sindhu Gangadharan (SVP & MD, SAP Labs India), illuminating the pivotal subjects of the SAP Community, Learning, Artificial Intelligence, and more. The event was nothing less than a feast for tech enthusiasts, featuring 56 deep-dive lecture sessions, ~120 speakers, six hands-on, and three interactive 'Ask Me Anything' booths. Running on eight parallel tracks, these sessions ensured a wide range of topics covered, providing a comprehensive learning experience for all attendees. I was fortunate to lead the content team, where I aided in strategizing and designing the content and working collaboratively with the team. We had seven tracks of lecture sessions, covering SAP BTP, Application Development - SAP Build, CAP & RAP, Integration, Data Analytics, Automation, SAP S/4HANA, SuccessFactors, SAP UI5, Fiori & Many other functional topics. Check out our agenda here: https://sitblrwebsite.vercel.app/

 

 

 

SAP BTP & Public Cloud at the Epicenter:

 

The SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) found significant prominence at this event. There were a total of 34 lecture sessions from SAP Employees, Customers & Partners on topics ranging from Application Development, Artificial intelligence, Automation, Integration and Data-to-Value, and also four hands-on practical interactions, and two specialized 'Ask Me Anything' booths that offered attendees valuable insights from SAP Product Experts. The BTP diamond game, held thrice for customers and partners, made its mark as an engaging highlight.

Some Of the interesting sessions which in the area of Automation, BTP, Intelligence and Public Cloud.

 

  1. SAP S/4HANA Migration Cockpit

           The SAP S/4HANA migration cockpit facilitates the transfer of business data from an SAP ERP source system to SAP S/4HANA. The SAP S/4HANA migration cockpit uses migration objects to identify and transfer the relevant data. A migration object describes how to migrate data for a specific business object to SAP S/4HANA. It contains information about the relevant source and target structures, as well as the relationships between these structures. It also contains mapping information for the relevant fields, as well as any rules used to convert values that are migrated from source fields to target fields. SAP provide predefined migration objects that you can use to transfer your data.

 

 

 Session PPT Link - https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/11DDrBuUpzNqd89DJyTZg7R9IfV_qgrI4/edit#slide=id.p43

 

  1. Model Enterprise Responsibilities for a Business User in Cloud - BTP

Come learn and experience how SAP Responsibility Management service in BTP is now helping BTP applications to model enterprise responsibilities for a business user easily. With it’s strong Query APIs and easy modelling of data , the RM service will soon become the central place for responsibility modelling as the need of managing responsibilities for a business user remains the same in Cloud and On Premise.

 

 

Session PPT Link -

  1. The New Age Intelligent Situation Handling in S4HANA

Situation Handling helps your business to run smoothly by automatically informing the right users about any situations that need their attention. This includes, for example, upcoming deadlines, delays, expiring contracts, and so on.

 

 

Session PPT Link - https://drive.google.com/drive/u/0/folders/10cPbe8TjGR71X9lg6b7vlotKv7P6Qswr

 

  1. Memory Consumption Dashboard for Information Lifecycle Management (ILM) Objects

Growing memory consumption leads to increased subscription costs for our customers when running SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition. Thanks to the enablement of ILM objects for the memory usage reporting, the ILM Advisor provides transparency on the ILM objects that drive the growth of memory consumption. Customers can now take an informed decision on triggering ILM actions to reduce the memory consumption by archiving data. You will learn about ILM Advisor app which provides a comprehensive overview of memory consumption based on ILM objects  in SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

 

 

 

Session PPT Link - https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1zStXKmC6aQ1R48Xo87zK4UEo7y-H_F4w/edit#slide=id.p40

  1. Build Extensions with developer extensibility in 3-System Landscape (3SL) using SAP Business Accelerator Hub

      Developer extensibility allows you to create development projects in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition solution. It gives you the opportunity to develop cloud-ready and upgrade-stable custom ABAP code on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public edition combining the benefits of custom ABAP code, with the required restrictions for Cloud readiness, and the SAP S/4HANA programming model to build SAP Fiori apps. Developer extensibility is only available in a 3-system landscape. The list of pre-built extensions that are available to be readily used by end-users can be explored on SAP Extensibility Explorer.

 

 

 

 

 

Session PPT Link - https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1Jg5EBPFI5OhMbbomzd4mKzVbBeQBSYd-/edit#slide=id.p40

 

Team Work:

The immense success of the event would not have been possible without the help of all our volunteers dedicated at every step. Their dedication and effort helped us execute one of the best SAP Inside Track events Bengaluru has ever seen. Their invaluable assistance was present at every event stage, from content curation to track management.

 

Sponsors

A massive thank you to our sponsors, without whom the SAP Inside Track Bengaluru wouldn't have been possible. Their ample financial and logistical support played a crucial part in the smooth running of the event. Not only did they provide us with the necessary backing to host a profuse range of sessions and interactive booths, but some also contributed their expertise as speakers. Their support was invaluable in making this event a grand success, and we extend our heartfelt thanks to them all. (Primus Techsystems, S2 Integrators, SAP Concur & SAP Press).

 



 

Gratitude

I sincerely thank Abhishek Chatterjee and Mahesh Pallavalli for the opportunity and their trust in me to be included in this big community event.

I am thankful to my leadership, Ashwini and Arun, for allowing me to go beyond the boundaries of my day-to-day activities. I also express my deep gratitude toward Sindhu Gangadharan, whose leadership and strategic insights were crucial in expanding the SAP Community's reach in this region to new heights.

entry.title: SAP Transportation Management Container Numbers ISO 6346
entry.content:
Do you know about this functionality in SAP TM.

ISO 6346 is an international standard covering the coding, identification and marking of intermodal (shipping) containers used within containerized intermodal freight transport by the International Organization for Standardization

Refer to; ISO 6346 - Wikipedia

This functionality supports you to ensure the Container Number insert in field Container/Registration Number is correct.

In case the number is not correct a Warning will appear:

If the Number is correct no Warning will appear.

To active it in SAP TM you need to follow this step:

Go to SPRO:

Once it’s open select the TU Group you’re using and in the Resource Class Select number 10 Container and save it.

With these steps the validation will be active for a user to get the validation of the ISO 6346

Technically SAP is using this Method /SCMTMS/CL_COMMON_BO_HELPER-CHECK_CONTAINER_ID

Refer to:

2737247 - TM: No check on container ID based on ISO 6346 - SAP for Me

Transportation Unit Resource | SAP Help Portal

Parameter Description | SAP Help Portal

Hope that can help you.

Thank you and regards!

Rogerio Lima.

entry.title: Re: API Specification for older releases
entry.content:
Hello,

Ok, when you make that call you are using /$count, but the openapi client that is generated from the specification uses $count=true, which doesn't work against my 2022 server:
/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_SALES_ORDER_SRV/A_SalesOrder?$count=true

as opposed to 

/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_SALES_ORDER_SRV/A_SalesOrder?$count

Second,

the 2023 spec returns something like this in json:

{
  "@count": 1000,
  "value": [
    {
      "CompanyCode": "stri",
      "FiscalYear": "stri",
      "AccountingDocument": "string",
      "AccountingDocumentItem": "str"
    }
  ]
}

But the 2022 returns something like this:

{
  "d": {
    "__count":  1000,
    "results": [
      {
        "CompanyCode": "stri",
        "FiscalYear": "stri",
        "AccountingDocument": "string",
        "AccountingDocumentItem": "str"
      }
    ]
  }
}

Of course I can make the calls using Postman, but what I am trying to do is using the generated classed through the openapi specification, and the generated classes for the openapi specification that you download for 2022 do not work with a 2022 server.

The issue is that the downloads for 2022 are actually identical to 2023, but the formats have changed.

entry.title: Generative AI-powered content recommendations in SAP Ariba Category Management!
entry.content:
As part of 2402 release, SAP Ariba Category Management introduced Generative AI enabled features that allows Category Managers to generate AI content recommendations for Category Segmentation, Market Dynamics, and Cost Structure tools.  The use of Gen AI contents will help Category Manager(s) to accelerate the Category Strategy and Plan development process by reducing the time required to update them manually.

How does it work?

SAP Ariba CatMan is uses SAP AI Core which is a service on SAP BTP Platform. SAP AI Core is the key to integrating artificial intelligence capabilities in SAP solutions. For CatMan Gen AI feature, SAP AI Core configured to consume OpenAI GPT-4 LLM.

Are there any customer specific or sensitive data being shared with OpenAI GPT-4?

No, SAP Ariba CatMan doesn’t share any customer specific or sensitive data with OpenAI during AI content generation. SAP Ariba CatMan sends below generic data to OpenAI to generate AI Content:

  1. Organization’s Industry
  2. Category Name
  3. Region
  4. Subcategories names if any
  5. Parent Category names
  6. Language

Things to note:

1. While Gen AI powered CatMan tools generate recommendations for Category Managers, its only a recommendation! If Category Manager decides to ignore and overwrite the auto generated content, it is totally fine and allowed in CatMan! 

2. Reference articles that are generated within each tool based on pre-configured questions that retrieve responses based on the tool +  user context.  This helps with educating users and ultimately onboarding users onto the various tools.

Let’s look in detail how to enable Gen AI feature in SAP Ariba CatMan:

Pre-requisites:

  1. You must have configured SAP Ariba Category Management Solution.
  2. You must have Category Administrator role collection.
  3. You must have signed agreement with SAP before enabling Gen AI feature in SAP Ariba CatMan

Step 1: Login to SAP Ariba CatMan. Click “Manage Configurations” UI app then go to “Integration” tab. Enable SAP Business AI service.

Step 2: “Strategy and Plan” tab -> Generative AI.  Enable AI feature for all available tools.

Step 3: General Settings -> Attributes -> Industry for Gen AI data. Select relevant industry based on your business line.

That’s it. You have now enabled Gen AI feature in SAP Ariba CatMan!

 

How does Gen AI feature look like in SAP Ariba CatMan UI?

In Category Segmentation tool:

Step 1:  Login to SAP Ariba CatMan as Category Manager and go to Develop Strategy and Plan then create new Strategy and Plan document.

 

Step 2: After creating new document, go to Category Segmentation and click on “Generate with AI.”

Step 3: If you are generating AI content for the first time, click “ok” on the “Acknowledgement.”

Step 4: SAP Ariba CatMan will generate business impact score, complexity of supply market score along with notes.

 

Gen AI generated Kraljic Matrix

 

Gen AI generated scores and notes

 

Additionally, it will also show the list of resources generated by SAP Business AI.

In Market Dynamics tool:

Step1: Click on “Market Dynamics” UI app.

Step2: Click on “Generate with AI”

Step 3: If you are generating AI content for the first time, click “ok” on the “Acknowledgement.”

Step 4: SAP Ariba CatMan will generate Porter’s five forces metric along with notes.

Gen AI recommended Porter's five forces chart

 

Gen AI generated score and notes for Market Dynamics

In Cost Structure tool:

Step1: Click on “Cost Structure” UI app.

Step2: Click on “Generate with AI”.

Step 3: If you are generating AI content for the first time, click “ok” on the “Acknowledgement.”

Step 4: SAP Ariba CatMan will generate Cost Structure components percentage and notes.

Gen AI generated Cost structure scores and notes

To conclude, these new Gen AI powered content recommendations will definitely help Category Managers accelerate their Category Strategy and Planning journey!

Happy learning!!

entry.title: Re: Charting on Print-Time Formulas or shared variables a...
entry.content:
Hi mmichael, not sure if that's one of my old posts but the technique is below. I can't attach the sample report to this post; there is no longer a way to attach report files as text in the forum.

Here's the workaround to charting those types of values:

  1. Insert a new subreport in a new section below your existing subreports.
  2. If you need to other data (i.e. more data than just your shared vars) then add that applicable datasource to this  new subreport.
  3. On the main report create formulae that bring back the shared vars from your previous subreports and place those in a section above the new subreport.
  4. Link the new subreport with those new formulae...make sure that each link is a "one way" link wherein the formula is not linked to any field in the subreport.
  5. Once the new subreport is linked you can use those link parameters in a chart (or crosstab or summary if needed).

Let me know if you have any questions.

entry.title: Re: Charting on Print-Time Formulas or shared variables a...
entry.content:
p.s. Another thing to consider is the use of SQL Expression fields as opposed to bringing back a single shared var value from a subreport. If you haven't used them before, SQL Expressions can be used in charts, summaries, and cross-tabs. This of course would be applicable to the DB connection that you're using in your main report. To do this, in your main report's Field Explorer, right click on SQL Expression Fields > New and add in your SQL. Note that you must return a single value.

e.g. 

(
    SELECT EXP(Sum(Log(`B1`.`Customer ID`))) 
    FROM `Customer` `B1` 
    WHERE `B1`.`Customer ID` <= `Customer`.`Customer ID`
)

entry.title: Re: SAP versions and modules
entry.content:
Hey there!

Absolutely, I'd be happy to help clear things up for you! Here's some friendly insight in points:

1. **SAP SD and SAP FICO**:
- Yes, you're right! SAP SD (Sales and Distribution) and SAP FICO (Finance and Controlling) are indeed core modules typically delivered with both SAP ERP and SAP S/4HANA.

2. **SAP FSCM**:
- SAP FSCM (Financial Supply Chain Management) is indeed an additional module that requires a separate license. It can be integrated with SAP S/4HANA but isn't delivered as part of the core package.

3. **SAP S/4HANA Modules**:
- SAP S/4HANA Sales, Finance, Collection Management, and Dispute Management are indeed available. They are part of the SAP S/4HANA suite and are designed to work seamlessly with the core system.
- These modules often have Fiori apps associated with them, providing a modern and intuitive user experience. They're usually included with the SAP S/4HANA package.

4. **SAP FSCM on SAP S/4HANA**:
- While SAP S/4HANA provides robust modules like Sales and Finance, SAP FSCM offers specialized functionalities focusing on optimizing financial processes and risk management. It's about complementing the core functionalities with additional features tailored to specific needs.

5. **Fiori in SAP S/4HANA**:
- Yes, Fiori is indeed delivered as a default for SAP S/4HANA. It provides a user-friendly and responsive interface, making tasks more intuitive and efficient.

6. **Learning SAP FSCM**:
-Checkout this playlist https://youtube.com/playlist?list=PLDD0pepTPuzBbwGjd8mW_xcUND2tVHGz_&si=AGuIQ11C0DCLUTjm

Hope this helps! Let me know if you have any more questions or need further assistance. Happy learning! 😊🌟

entry.title: Re: Plant wise Business Place with same GSTIN
entry.content:
Hello,

Absolutely, I can guide you through the process. Here's what you can do to accommodate your client's requirement:

1. **Business Place Configuration**:
- You can indeed create plant-specific business places under the same company code with the same GSTIN. This setup allows for separate reporting while maintaining consistency in tax filings.
- In SAP, you'll need to create a new business place for the new plant. You can do this using transaction code **OX18**. Specify the plant and assign the same GSTIN as the existing business place.

2. **Configuration Steps**:
- First, navigate to transaction code **OX18**.
- Select the country IN (India) and the company code for which you want to create a new business place.
- Enter the new business place details, including the plant and the same GSTIN as the existing business place.
- Save the configuration.

3. **Accounting Impact**:
- From an accounting perspective, creating a new business place under the same company code shouldn't have significant effects. The transactions will still be captured under the same company code.
- However, it's essential to ensure that all relevant tax codes and accounting configurations are correctly set up for the new business place to avoid any discrepancies in reporting or compliance.

4. **Verification**:
- Once the configuration is done, it's crucial to verify the setup thoroughly. Test transactions in the new plant to ensure that tax calculations and reporting are functioning correctly.
- Additionally, cross-check the reporting requirements with your client to confirm that the new setup meets their internal reporting needs.

By following these steps, you should be able to create a new plant with a separate business place under the same company code while using the same GSTIN. If you have any further questions or need assistance with specific configuration steps, feel free to ask!

entry.title: Re: filling up free capacity with producing requests earl...
entry.content:
Hi Jo,

Absolutely, I understand your requirement and there are ways to address it within SAP IBP. Here's a solution for you:

1. **Adjusting Production Horizon**:
- In SAP IBP, you can adjust the production horizon to bring forward the production of requirements within a defined time frame. This can be achieved by adjusting planning parameters such as lead times or scheduling policies.

2. **Utilizing Free Capacity**:
- By bringing forward production, you can effectively utilize the free capacity in production without affecting employee schedules.

3. **Customization in IBP**:
- This customization can be done using planning profiles and scheduling parameters in the IBP Order-Based Planning application.

4. **Fiori App for Customization**:
- The Fiori app you can use for this customization is the "Maintain Planning Profiles" app. Within this app, you can define planning profiles and set parameters related to production horizon, lead times, and scheduling policies.

5. **Defining Time Horizon**:
- Within the planning profile, you can define the time horizon within which you want to bring forward production. This allows you to specify the period during which requirements should be produced earlier to utilize free capacity.

6. **Impact on Inventory**:
- Adjusting the production horizon may result in increased inventory levels as goods may stay in the warehouse longer. However, this can be managed through inventory optimization techniques within SAP IBP.

By leveraging these capabilities in SAP IBP and customizing the planning profiles accordingly, you can effectively bring forward production to utilize free capacity while ensuring that employees are not affected. Let me know if you need further assistance or clarification on any of these points.

Best regards,
H

entry.title: Re: How to read a pdf file from desktop to internal table...
entry.content:
Certainly! To read a PDF file from the presentation layer (local file system) into an internal table in SAP ABAP, you can follow these steps:

1. **Upload PDF File to SAP Presentation Server**:
- Use the transaction `CG3Z` or similar to upload the PDF file from your local system to the presentation server (the application server of SAP system).

2. **Read PDF File and Populate Internal Table**:
- Use the function module `GUI_UPLOAD` to read the content of the PDF file into an internal table. Ensure that the internal table structure matches the content of your PDF file.
- Below is an example code snippet:

DATA: lt_pdf_content TYPE TABLE OF char255,
lv_file_name TYPE string,
lv_path TYPE string,
lv_rc TYPE i.

lv_file_name = 'YOUR_PDF_FILE.pdf'. "Replace with your PDF file name
lv_path = 'YOUR_PATH/'. "Replace with the path where the PDF file is located

CALL FUNCTION 'GUI_UPLOAD'
EXPORTING
filename = lv_path
filetype = 'BIN'
TABLES
data_tab = lt_pdf_content
EXCEPTIONS
file_open_error = 1
file_read_error = 2
no_batch = 3
gui_refuse_filetransfer = 4
invalid_type = 5
no_authority = 6
unknown_error = 7
bad_data_format = 8
header_not_allowed = 9
separator_not_allowed = 10
header_too_long = 11
unknown_dp_error = 12
access_denied = 13
dp_out_of_memory = 14
disk_full = 15
dp_timeout = 16
file_not_found = 17
dataprovider_exception = 18
control_flush_error = 19
OTHERS = 20.

IF sy-subrc EQ 0.
WRITE: 'PDF file uploaded successfully.'.
ELSE.
WRITE: 'Error while uploading PDF file:', sy-subrc.
ENDIF.

 

3. **Perform Operations on Internal Table**:
- Once the PDF content is loaded into the internal table (`lt_pdf_content` in the example above), you can perform any required operations on the data.

4. **Processing PDF Content**:
- PDF content might not be in a structured format, so you may need to parse it accordingly. This might involve splitting the content, searching for specific patterns, or using third-party libraries for PDF parsing if necessary.

5. **Error Handling**:
- Ensure to handle any exceptions that might occur during the file upload process (`sy-subrc` values).

By following these steps, you should be able to read the content of a PDF file from the presentation layer into an internal table in SAP ABAP and perform necessary operations.

entry.title: Navigating the Skies: Optimizing Supply Chain Management in the Travel Industry with SAP
entry.content:
SAP, a global leader in enterprise software, offers a comprehensive suite of solutions tailored to the unique needs of the travel industry. By leveraging SAP's advanced technology, travel companies can streamline their supply chain processes, enhance operational efficiency, and deliver exceptional experiences to customers from booking to journey completion.

One of the key challenges in the travel industry is managing complex networks of suppliers, partners, and vendors while maintaining high standards of quality and reliability. For a travel site serving the faith community , this could be a very specific set of partners. SAP's integrated platform provides a unified view of the entire supply chain, allowing companies to track and manage every aspect of their operations in real-time. With SAP solutions, travel companies can optimize inventory management, procurement, and logistics, ensuring that the right resources are available at the right time and place.

Timeliness is of the essence in the travel industry, where even minor delays can have ripple effects across the entire supply chain. SAP's advanced analytics and predictive capabilities enable companies to anticipate demand fluctuations, identify potential bottlenecks, and proactively address issues before they escalate. By harnessing the power of data-driven insights, travel companies can optimize route planning, allocate resources more efficiently, and minimize the risk of disruptions.

Moreover, SAP's cloud-based solutions offer scalability and flexibility, allowing travel companies to adapt to changing market dynamics and seasonal demand fluctuations with ease. Whether it's scaling up operations during peak travel seasons or optimizing resources during off-peak periods, SAP provides the agility and resilience needed to stay ahead in a dynamic environment.

In addition to optimizing operational efficiency, SAP solutions also play a crucial role in enhancing collaboration and communication across the supply chain ecosystem. By providing a centralized platform for collaboration, travel companies can streamline communication with suppliers, track performance metrics, and ensure compliance with regulatory requirements. Whether it's coordinating with airlines, hotels, or ground transportation providers, SAP enables seamless collaboration and integration across the entire value chain.

Furthermore, SAP's commitment to innovation ensures that travel companies stay ahead of the curve in an ever-evolving industry landscape. From emerging technologies such as artificial intelligence and blockchain to next-generation analytics and Internet of Things (IoT) solutions, SAP continues to push the boundaries of what's possible in supply chain management.

In conclusion, optimizing supply chain management is critical for success in the travel industry, and SAP provides the tools and technologies needed to achieve this goal. By leveraging SAP's advanced solutions, travel companies can ensure timely delivery of services, minimize disruptions, and deliver exceptional experiences to customers. In an industry where every moment counts, SAP empowers travel companies to navigate the skies with confidence and agility, driving sustainable growth and competitive advantage.

entry.title: Re: Delivery without PGI
entry.content:
Hey, 

In SAP Business One, creating an outbound delivery without performing a Post Goods Issue (PGI) is not a standard functionality. In SAP Business One, the outbound delivery process typically involves creating a delivery document followed by a PGI to confirm that the goods have physically left the warehouse.

However, you might be able to achieve a similar outcome through customization or development using the SAP Business One Software Development Kit (SDK) if your business process requires such functionality. Customization would involve writing custom code to bypass the PGI step, but this would need to be carefully implemented to ensure that it aligns with your business requirements and does not compromise inventory accuracy or other processes.

 

best regards

Stefan

SAP Community

entry.title: CRM Basic Technical Info for ABAPers
entry.content:
1. UIs
    1. SAP GUI - Still used
    2. People Centric UI - SAP CRM 3.1, 4.0, 5.0. Poor reception from users.
    3. IC Web Client - SAP CRM 4.0, 5.0. This became the foundation for Web Client
    4. Web Client - CRM 2006s, Still used.
      1. Application Enhancement Tool(AET)
  2. CRM Middleware
    1. Set of code in CRM and SAP ERP to exchange data between the two systems.
    2. Based on qRFC
    3. Uses Bdocs
      1. Similar to IDOCs.
    4. OPEN FI user exits
      1. Post Middleware configuration, data gets transferred to OPEN FI user exits which makes a special RFC call that transfers data via a structure called Business Document(BDoc).
  3. Development Tools
    1. Easy Enhancement Workbench(EEWB) 
      1. Old enhancement tool
    2. Application Enhancement Tool
      1. New enhancement tool from SAP CRM 7.0.
      2. Replacement of EEWB.
    3. External Interface(XIF) Adaptor
      1. Sits on top of CRM middleware.
      2. Series of remote FMs and IDOCs that translate a data structure into Bdoc for inbound processing or outbound processing.
    4. Post Processing Framework(PPF)

 

 

 

Business Partner

SD11

Transaction

To view CRM Business Partner Data Model

Data Model PRM_BP

BAPI_BUPA*

BAPI

All BUPA related BAPIs

 

BAPI_BUPA_SEARCH

BAPI

Search Business Partner

 

BAPI_BUPA_CENTRAL_GETDETAIL

BAPI

Get Business Partner details

 

BAPI_BUPA_CREATE_FROM_DATA

BAPI

Create Business Partner

 

 

 

 

 

General Data

Table

General Data

 

BUT000-PARTNER

Field

10 Digit alphanumeric primary key

 

BUT000-PARTNER_GUID

Field

To link with other CRM tables

 

BUT000-TYPE

Field

Person, Organization, Group

Field

For filed control

 

BUT000-BU_GROUP

Field

For number range

 

CI_EEW_BUT000

Include

Include for enhancing BP with new attributes

 

 

 

 

 

Sales Data

 

 

CRMM_BUT_LINK*

Link a set of attribute data to business partner

 

 

CRMM_BUT_SET*

unique set of attributes for a given period

 

 

BAPI_BUPA_FRG<NUMBER>

BAPIs for each set of attributes

 

 

 

 

Address Data

BUT020

Table

Address Data

 

BUPA_ADDRESSES_GET

Function Module

Get the list of addresses for a business partner

 

BUPA_ADDRESSES_GET_DETAIL

Get the detail of a particular address

 

BUPR_CONTP_ADDRESSES_GET
